ORDER RE: MOTION TO DISMISS

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ANITA SCHEFFEL'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DISMISSAL PURSUANT TO CCP 473(B). ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION IS GRANTED. MR. TISHGART MUST PAY ATTORNEYS FOR DEFENDANT $2,352 AS REASONABLE COMPENSATORY LEGAL FEES. CCP SECTION 473(B) (THIS AMOUNT DOES NOT INCLUDE TODAY'S APPEARANCE.) DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO DISMISS THE COMPLAINT IS SET FOR HEARING ON MAY 28, 2009. REPLY IS DUE MAY 21, 2009. COUNSEL WILL NOTICE THE MOTION AND PROVIDE A COPY OF THE ALL THE MOVING PAPERS, INCLUDING THE OPPOSITION. DEFENSE COUNSEL TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

calendar.matters:
Re-Ntc Re Deft Motion To Dismiss
Off Calendar Jun-04-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ROYAL CARIBBEAN CRUISES, LTD. Re-notice Regarding Motion To Dismiss. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to dismiss is granted. The forum selection clause is enforceable. See Carnival Cruise Lines vs. Shute (1991) 499 U.S. 585. (302/CWW/JW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

Notice Of Hearing Re: Defendant Royal Caribbean Cruises, Ltd'S Motion To Dismiss
Off Calendar May-28-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Defendant Royal Caribbean Cruises, Ltd's Motion to Dismiss. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Matter to be heard June 4, 2009 per agreement of counsel. Counsel has filed a re-notice of Motion to Dismiss for June 4, 2009. Counsel are to deliver a courtesy set for all moving papers. =(302/CWW)

Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Dismissal Pursuant To Ccp 473(B)
Off Calendar May-07-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF ANITA SCHEFFEL'S Motion to Set Aside Dismissal Pursuant To CCP 473(B). Appearances: Kenneth B. Tishgart, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 461-4690, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; Ronald Losch, Esq. of Kaye, Rose, & Partners, LLP (415) 433-6555, appearing for Defendant Royal Caribbean Cruises, Ltd., and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is granted. Mr. Tishgart must pay attorneys for defendant $2,352 as reasonable compensatory legal fees. CCP section 473(b) (This amount does not include today's appearance.) Defendant's Motion to Dismiss the Complaint is set for Hearing on May 28, 2009. Reply is due May 21, 2009. Counsel will notice the motion and provide a copy of the all the moving papers, including the opposition. Defense counsel to prepare Order. (302/CWW/JW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
